name: Koulourakia (Greek Easter Cookies)
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg Yolk beaten with 1 Tablespoon Milk for glaze
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Beat together the butter and powdered sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add the egg yolk, brandy (or milk), and vanilla; mix well.
4. Gradually add flour, baking powder, and salt, mixing after each addition.
5. Using a rounded teaspoon of dough, roll each piece on a lightly floured surface until it forms a 6-inch rope.
6. Bring the ends together to form a hairpin shape, then gently twist 2-3 times.
7. Lightly pinch the ends together.
8. Arrange the twists about 1 inch apart on greased baking sheets.
9. Brush with egg glaze and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
10. Bake for 10-13 minutes or until golden.
11. Cool on wire racks.
12. Store airtight at room temperature for up to two weeks. Freeze for longer storage.
